summary: Terrapin jam, stormy magic and soaring moon in Vegas
blurb: The show shines with a thunder‑charged Terrapin Station > Spanish Jam sequence and an electrifying Standing On The Moon. Jerry’s guitar is strong for most of the night while Mickey and Bill drive a powerful percussion wall. Overall a solid mid‑’90s performance worth hearing.
review: On May 30, 1992 at the Sam Boyd Silver Bowl, the Dead opened the evening with a crisp Jack Straw → Sugaree that set a relaxed tone before diving into a second set that became a storm‑lit spectacle. The highlight was undoubtedly the Terrapin Station suite, which exploded into an expansive Spanish Jam, amplified by Mickey Hart’s thunderous drumming and Bill Kreutzmann’s tight grooves. A lightning‑charged Help > Slip > Franklin's sequence (captured despite some tape distortion) segued into a soaring Standing On The Moon that seemed to chase the desert sky itself. Jerry Garcia’s guitar was praised as strong and soulful on most numbers, though one soundboard notes his mix sat low at times. Phil Lesh supplied standout bass fills throughout the jams, while Vince Welnick’s keyboards earned mixed reactions—some fans loved the cha‑cha texture, others called it “Fisher‑Price.” The first set also featured energetic renditions of Jack Straw and Sugaree, rounding out a night that feels both magical and grounded in solid playing.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the Terrapin/Spanish Jam, Standing On The Moon and weather‑enhanced moments; only one mixed note about Garcia’s low mix, no consistent negative feedback – strong positive consensus yields a high sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard that captures the key jams (Terrapin, Spanish Jam, lightning‑charged Help/Slip/Franklin’s) with clear instrument separation despite occasional distortion peaks.

key_highlights: Thunderous Terrapin Station > Spanish Jam sequence
Soaring Standing On The Moon under stormy skies
Powerful dual‑drum percussion driving the second set

band_performance.Jerry: Strong, soulful guitar work praised throughout; a few notes of tentative low‑mix in one recording but overall solid playing and healthy vocals on ballads.
band_performance.Phil: Provides standout lead‑bass fills during the Terrapin jam and adds depth to the rain‑and‑snow vibe, keeping the groove tight.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers energetic rhythm guitar, highlighted on Jack Straw and contributes to the drive of the first set.
band_performance.Brent: Vince Welnick’s keyboards receive mixed feedback – some fans love his cha‑cha riff and texture, others describe the tone as weak; nonetheless he adds color to the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Percussion shines, especially in the Terrapin/Spanish Jam and storm‑themed sections, delivering a powerful, syncopated drive.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming pairs tightly with Mickey, creating a dynamic, thunderous backbone throughout the second set.
